fault_hypothesis: The rate limit check on line 66 uses a strict greater-than comparison (`>`), but it should use greater-than-or-equal-to (`>=`) to deny checkouts when the number of recent events is exactly at the limit.

constraints.intent: The number of recent events should not be exactly at the limit, as this boundary case is handled incorrectly.
If the code decides to allow the checkout, it must be because the number of recent events is strictly less than the limit.
A checkout should not be allowed if there is no remaining capacity.
